steps.title: Identify the condition for no solution
steps.content:
A system of linear equations has no solution when the two equations represent parallel lines — they have the same slope but different y-intercepts. Simplify the given equation by dividing all terms by 30:

$$30x = 1,800y - 3,600$$
$$x = 60y - 120$$

steps.title: Check choice A for parallel line structure
steps.content:
Multiply choice A by 30 to match the form of the simplified first equation:

$$\frac{1}{30}x = 2y$$
$$x = 60y$$

Both equations now show the relationship $x = 60y + k$. The first has $k = -120$, and choice A has $k = 0$. Since they share the same coefficients for $x$ and $y$ but differ in the constant term, they represent lines with the same slope but different y-intercepts—parallel lines with no intersection. That matches choice A — A is correct.
